steps.title: Sterilize filtration unit and other equipment
steps.instruction: Sterilize filtration unit and other equipment.

steps.title: Moisten CN support filters and PC...
steps.instruction: Moisten CN support filters and PC filters (shiny side of the PC-filter should face upwards) in sterile water.

steps.title: Place moistened CN support filters and...
steps.instruction: Place moistened CN support filters and PC filters (shiny side up) in the filtration unit.

steps.title: Check planarity of filtration unit and...
steps.instruction: Check planarity of filtration unit and filters with a water-level to allow for an even distribution of planktonic cells on the filter surface.

steps.title: Filter water sample by applying a gentle vacuum (approx
steps.instruction: Filter water sample by applying a gentle vacuum (approx. 150 mbar). 100 ml of water is sufficient for cell numbers of around 10 5 ml -1 . undetermined, water sample VACUUM FILTRATION 150 mbar

steps.title: Remove vacuum and overlay filter with...
steps.instruction: Remove vacuum and overlay filter with 4 ml of 3% PFA fixative (mix 3 ml of 4% PFA solution with 1 ml 1x PBS) for 20 min. undetermined, 3% PFA fixative 00:20:00, fixation

steps.title: Remove fixative by applying a vacuum
steps.instruction: Remove fixative by applying a vacuum.

steps.title: Wash cells with 3 ml 1x PBS
steps.instruction: Wash cells with 3 ml 1x PBS. undetermined, 1xPBS

steps.title: Wash cells with sterile water
steps.instruction: Wash cells with sterile water.

steps.title: Repeat washing with 1x PBS and water two times
steps.instruction: Repeat washing with 1x PBS and water two times. undetermined, 1xPBS

steps.title: Remove filter from filtration unit with...
steps.instruction: Remove filter from filtration unit with sterile tweezers and place it on a Whatman paper for drying (shiny side up) in a dust-free environment (e.g. petri dish).

steps.title: Label shiny side of the filter...
steps.instruction: Label shiny side of the filter with a pencil and store at -20°C until hybridization. Filters can be stored frozen for several months without apparent loss of hybridization signal. Negative control: Repeat procedure with sterile water.

steps.title: Prepare 2 ml of hybridization buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare 2 ml of hybridization buffer. undetermined, hybridization buffer

steps.title: Add 3 µl of probe working...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µl of probe working solution to 27 µl of hybridization buffer; keep probe solutions dark and on ice. undetermined, probe working solution undetermined, hybridization buffer

steps.title: Place filter on a microscopic slide...
steps.instruction: Place filter on a microscopic slide (shiny side up) and cut it with a sterile scalpel in four sections.

steps.title: Label filter sections with a pencil
steps.instruction: Label filter sections with a pencil.

steps.title: Place filter section(s) on a microscopic...
steps.instruction: Place filter section(s) on a microscopic slide (shiny side up); several filter sections can be placed on one slide and hybridized simultaneously with the same probe.

steps.title: For the preparation of an equilibrated...
steps.instruction: For the preparation of an equilibrated chamber, place a piece of tissue into a 50 ml tube and moisten it with the remaining hybridization buffer.

steps.title: Drop 30 ml hybridization solution on...
steps.instruction: Drop 30 ml hybridization solution on a filter section and place the slide horizontally into the 50 ml tube and close the lid tightly. undetermined, hybridization solution

steps.title: Incubate at 46°C for at least...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 46°C for at least 90 min in a hybridization oven. Undetermined, hybridization oven 01:30:00, hybridization

steps.title: Prepare 50 ml of washing buffer...
steps.instruction: Prepare 50 ml of washing buffer in a 50 ml plastic tube and pre-heat at 48°C. undetermined, washing buffer Undetermined, pre-heating

steps.title: Quickly transfer filter sections into warm...
steps.instruction: Quickly transfer filter sections into warm washing buffer and incubate for 15 min at 48°C in the water bath. Undetermined, water bath 00:15:00, incubation

steps.title: Pick filter sections with sterile tweezers...
steps.instruction: Pick filter sections with sterile tweezers and rinse in sterile double-distilled water for several seconds.

steps.title: Dry filter sections on Whatman paper
steps.instruction: Dry filter sections on Whatman paper.

steps.title: For counterstaining with a nucleic acid...
steps.instruction: For counterstaining with a nucleic acid dye, place filter sections on a slide, cover with 80 µl of SYBR Green I (Schmid et al. 2000) or DAPI solution (Hicks et al. 1992), and incubate for 10 minutes on ice in the dark. Wash filter sections for several seconds in cold 50% ethanol, rinse in double-distilled water, and dry on Whatman paper. undetermined, SYBR Green I Undetermined, on ice 00:10:00, incubation in the dark

steps.title: For microscopy, place dry filter section...
steps.instruction: For microscopy, place dry filter section on a glass slide and apply one to two drops of antifadent on the filter.

steps.title: Put a microscope cover slip on...
steps.instruction: Put a microscope cover slip on top and wait until the antifadent has spread over the filter. NOTE: too much antifadent can blur the microscope image, avoid bubbles.

steps.title: Observe the sample under an epifluorescence...
steps.instruction: Observe the sample under an epifluorescence microscope or confocal laser scanning microscope equipped with suitable filters or lasers, respectively.

steps.title: Slides embedded in antifadent can be...
steps.instruction: Slides embedded in antifadent can be stored at 4°C (do not freeze) for several days before the probe-conferred fluorescence begins to decline. Alternatively, the antifadent can be removed with water and the dried slides can be stored at -20°C for longer periods. NOTE: Probe-conferred fluorescence fades much more rapidly than DAPI fluorescence.

steps.title: Microscopy and manual quantification of absolute and relative cell numbers
steps.instruction: Microscopy and manual quantification of absolute and relative cell numbers. Absolute total cell numbers are determined by manual counting of DAPI-stained cells in at least 50 randomly chosen microscopic sections. For analyses of relative cell numbers of individual bacterial populations, DAPI-staining is combined with FISH. For each hybridization experiment, DAPI- and probe-stained cells in 20 randomly chosen microscopic sections are counted at a magnification of x400 respectively x1000. (NOTE for weak fluorescence signals: To avoid fluorescence bleaching of probe-labeled cells during DAPI examination, each microscopic field should be first viewed with the FLUOS filter, thereafter with the Cy3 filter before finally switching to the DAPI filter). At least 500 DAPI-stained cells should be counted to obtain a counting error < ±5%. All oligonucleotide probe counts are corrected by subtracting the counts obtained with the negative control probe NON338. The ratio of the number of those cells labeled by the rRNA-targeted oligonucleotide probe to the total number of bacterial cells stained by DAPI is calculated for each microscopic section.
